summary: Plan 15 is implementation-ready after its dependencies close. It correctly treats Bedrock model support, prompt-attack shielding, and harmful-content moderation as distinct controls; adds two explicit model-independent ApplyGuardrail transforms over one audited default-chain client; fails closed on response/policy drift; gives the target LLM an enabled/preferred provider-neutral control inventory without exposing resolved configuration or auto-inserting topology; and makes task-role IAM, audit-first ordering, redaction, and safe/intervened live outcomes executable.

review_basis.baseline: Current Azure prompt-shield/content-safety transforms, audited HTTP/LLM client order, composer shield availability and assistance, Plan 09 Bedrock scope, AWS Plans 00/03/10/12, live Filigree DAG, and current Amazon Bedrock Guardrails/ApplyGuardrail documentation were inspected.

warnings.detail: The Plan 15 step is pending, unassigned, and blocked by Plan 06, complete Plan 08A, and the shared verification baseline. This review did not claim or transition it.
Bedrock's listed harmful-content categories omit Azure Content Safety self_harm. Operators need an explicit residual-risk decision or another control before claiming equivalent coverage.
Functionality is required and live-accepted, but composer topology changes still require explicit user or operator-policy authority. Recommendations alone do not prove placement.
Unit Stubber tests cannot prove the provisioned Guardrail contains the required immutable policy family. Plan 12 must execute safe/intervened fixtures inside the candidate task with zero skips.

resolved_findings.resolution: Made Plan 09's content_filter mapping explicitly insufficient and placed shielding in a separate model-independent ApplyGuardrail slice.
Preserved distinct prompt-shield and content-safety plugin names, assessment families, outputs, assistance vocabulary, and graph-placement tests.
Added numeric immutable versions, default credential chain only, credential/profile/role/endpoint rejection, strict full-response parsing, and fail-closed policy/category drift behavior.
Added audit-first records, payload-free telemetry/public errors, property tests for malicious provider shapes, and forbidden-sentinel verification.
Added task-role ApplyGuardrail IAM, live safe/intervened prompt/content lanes, negative version/permission/schema cases, and explicit self-harm residual documentation.

Made shield discovery explicit: one request-scoped inventory derives usable controls from registration, enable flags, validated config, probe state, and secret references; the target LLM selects only an advertised operator-preferred implementation and sees only approved reference placeholders.

verification: Repository inspection confirmed only Azure prompt/content transforms exist and composer availability is Azure-specific.
Plan 09 contains no Guardrail configuration or independent shielding proof.
AWS documentation confirms Guardrails include PROMPT_ATTACK and harmful-content filters and ApplyGuardrail operates without model invocation.
The plan, specification, cross-plan dependencies, and Filigree graph now make both required AWS controls explicit.
This review did not implement Plan 15, start/claim/close its issue, commit, deploy, or disturb unrelated worktree changes.
